reasoning: The March 8 coalition, as explicitly defined in the resolution criteria, excludes the Free Patriotic Movement (FPM) unless they run on joint lists. FPM officially ended its alliance with Hezbollah in October 2024. Without FPM's ~17 seats, the remaining parties (Hezbollah, Amal, Marada, SSNP, Tashnag) mathematically possess a very low ceiling. Shiite representation is strictly capped at 27 seats, meaning Hezbollah and Amal can only contribute a maximum of 27 seats even if they win every single one. Tashnag (Armenian) usually wins ~3 seats, and Marada/SSNP rarely win more than 2-4 combined. Together, their core block caps out at fewer than 35 seats. To secure a 65-seat majority, they would need an additional 30+ seats from independent/Sunni/Christian candidates openly running on their explicitly endorsed lists. Given Hezbollah’s severe organizational degradation (loss of Nasrallah, 3800 fighters, and Syrian regime support), widespread opposition (reflected in the 2025 election of President Joseph Aoun), and immense popular blame for the 2024 war's devastation, a massive surge of over 30 cross-sectarian independent seats joining Hezbollah's lists is virtually impossible. The probability of reaching 65 seats under these conditions is minimal.
